statement: For a linear endomorphism on a finite-dimensional space, some iterate has
lean_statement:
theorem exists_pow_range_ker_isCompl
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V] [FiniteDimensional K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) :
    ∃ n : Nat, IsCompl (LinearMap.range (f ^ n)) (LinearMap.ker (f ^ n))
proof:
`range (f^n) ⊕ ker (f^n) = V`.

Proof: consider the descending chain of subspaces
`range f ⊇ range (f^2) ⊇ range (f^3) ⊇ ...`. In finite dimension this chain stabilizes, so choose
`n` with `range (f^n) = range (f^(n+1))`. Then `f` maps `range (f^n)` onto itself, hence (by finite
dimension) it is injective on `range (f^n)`, and so is `f^n`.
If `x ∈ range (f^n) ∩ ker (f^n)`, then `f^n x = 0` and injectivity of `f^n` on `range (f^n)` gives
`x = 0`. Thus the intersection is trivial. By rank-nullity for `f^n`,
`dim(range (f^n)) + dim(ker (f^n)) = dim(V)`, so the sum of these subspaces has dimension `dim(V)`,
hence equals `V`. Therefore `range (f^n)` and `ker (f^n)` are complementary, as required.
